Title: Network Device Inventory Browse - Display Table with All Columns by Endpoint

Step Action: Log in to OT Integrity Corp as a valid ICS/OT Security Engineer user.
Step Expected: User is authenticated and the application home page is displayed successfully.

Step Action: Navigate to the Network Device Inventory Browse page.
Step Expected: The Network Device Inventory Browse page is displayed.

Step Action: Verify the inventory table is displayed.
Step Expected: The inventory table is visible with data loaded.

Step Action: Verify the following columns are present in the table: Endpoint; # (Number of Components); Manufacturer; Type; Product; Version; Site Location; IP Address; MAC Address; Integrity Asset; Site Hierarchy Location; Model Number; Serial Number; Descriptor.
Step Expected: All 14 columns are visible in the table header row: Endpoint, #, Manufacturer, Type, Product, Version, Site Location, IP Address, MAC Address, Integrity Asset, Site Hierarchy Location, Model Number, Serial Number, Descriptor.

Step Action: Verify that the column previously labeled 'Location' is now labeled 'Site Hierarchy Location'.
Step Expected: The column header reads 'Site Hierarchy Location' and not 'Location'.

Step Action: Verify each row in the table represents a network device endpoint with data populated in the available columns.
Step Expected: Each row displays relevant network device data across the visible columns.

Step Action: Successfully verified the Network Device Inventory Browse page displays a table with all required columns for network devices including the renamed Site Hierarchy Location column.

Title: Network Device Inventory Browse - Browse By Network Device Manufacturer

Step Action: Log in to OT Integrity Corp as a valid user.
Step Expected: User is authenticated and the application is displayed.

Step Action: Navigate to the Network Device Inventory Browse page.
Step Expected: The Network Device Inventory Browse page is displayed.

Step Action: Locate the Browse By control and select 'Network Device Manufacturer'.
Step Expected: The table updates to display endpoints grouped or filtered by Network Device Manufacturer.

Step Action: Verify a list of endpoints is displayed.
Step Expected: A list of network device endpoints is shown in the table.

Step Action: Locate the filter panel and verify a Product filter is available grouped by Vendor.
Step Expected: A Product filter grouped by Vendor is present and accessible.

Step Action: Locate the filter panel and verify an Endpoint Type filter is available.
Step Expected: An Endpoint Type filter is present and accessible.

Step Action: Select a value from the Product (grouped by Vendor) filter.
Step Expected: The table updates to show only endpoints matching the selected product.

Step Action: Clear the Product filter and select a value from the Endpoint Type filter.
Step Expected: The table updates to show only endpoints matching the selected endpoint type.

Step Action: Successfully verified that Browse By Network Device Manufacturer displays a list of endpoints and the Product (grouped by Vendor) and Endpoint Type filters apply correctly to endpoint properties.

Title: Network Device Inventory Browse - Browse By Component

Step Action: Log in to OT Integrity Corp as a valid user.
Step Expected: User is authenticated and the application is displayed.

Step Action: Navigate to the Network Device Inventory Browse page.
Step Expected: The Network Device Inventory Browse page is displayed.

Step Action: Locate the Browse By control and select 'Component'.
Step Expected: The table updates to display endpoints with their component-level data.

Step Action: Verify a list of endpoints is displayed.
Step Expected: A list of endpoints is shown in the table.

Step Action: Locate the filter panel and verify a Product filter is available grouped by Vendor.
Step Expected: A Product filter grouped by Vendor is present.

Step Action: Locate the filter panel and verify a Component Type filter is available.
Step Expected: A Component Type filter is present and accessible.

Step Action: Select a value from the Product (grouped by Vendor) filter.
Step Expected: The table updates to show only endpoints whose components match the selected product.

Step Action: Clear the Product filter and select a value from the Component Type filter.
Step Expected: The table updates to show only endpoints whose components match the selected component type.

Step Action: Successfully verified that Browse By Component displays a list of endpoints and the Product (grouped by Vendor) and Component Type filters apply correctly to endpoint components.

Title: Network Device Inventory Browse - Widget Displayed and Remains Static

Step Action: Log in to OT Integrity Corp as a valid user.
Step Expected: User is authenticated and the application is displayed.

Step Action: Navigate to the Network Device Inventory Browse page.
Step Expected: The Network Device Inventory Browse page is displayed.

Step Action: Locate the widget displayed in the top-left area of the page.
Step Expected: A widget is visible in the top-left position of the page.

Step Action: Note the value(s) displayed in the widget upon page load.
Step Expected: Widget value(s) are recorded as a baseline.

Step Action: Apply a filter from the filter panel (e.g. select a Manufacturer or Product filter).
Step Expected: The table updates based on the filter selection.

Step Action: Verify the widget value(s) in the top-left have not changed.
Step Expected: The widget still displays the same value(s) recorded in step 4.

Step Action: Change the Browse By selection (e.g. switch between Manufacturer and Component).
Step Expected: The table view updates accordingly.

Step Action: Verify the widget value(s) have still not changed.
Step Expected: The widget continues to display the original value(s) from page load.

Step Action: Successfully verified that the top-left widget is static and non-interactive; its value does not change based on filter selections or Browse By changes after the page is loaded.

Title: Network Device Inventory Browse - Each Endpoint Has a Link to Display Components

Step Action: Log in to OT Integrity Corp as a valid user.
Step Expected: User is authenticated and the application is displayed.

Step Action: Navigate to the Network Device Inventory Browse page.
Step Expected: The Network Device Inventory Browse page is displayed with the inventory table loaded.

Step Action: Locate an endpoint row in the inventory table.
Step Expected: An endpoint row is visible in the table.

Step Action: Verify the endpoint row contains a link or clickable control to display its components.
Step Expected: A link or clickable control associated with viewing components is visible on the endpoint row.

Step Action: Click the component link on an endpoint row.
Step Expected: The components associated with that endpoint are displayed (e.g. in an expanded row or detail view).

Step Action: Verify the displayed components belong to the selected endpoint.
Step Expected: Component data shown is relevant to the endpoint that was clicked.

Step Action: Return to the main inventory table and verify other endpoint rows also have component links.
Step Expected: All endpoint rows in the table have a visible component link.

Step Action: Successfully verified that each endpoint in the Network Device Inventory Browse table has a link that displays its associated components.

Title: Network Device Inventory Browse - Filters Are Searchable

Step Action: Log in to OT Integrity Corp as a valid user.
Step Expected: User is authenticated and the application is displayed.

Step Action: Navigate to the Network Device Inventory Browse page.
Step Expected: The Network Device Inventory Browse page is displayed.

Step Action: Open the filter panel.
Step Expected: The filter panel is visible with available filter options.

Step Action: Click on the Product filter dropdown or search field.
Step Expected: The Product filter control becomes active.

Step Action: Type a partial product name into the filter search field.
Step Expected: The filter list narrows to display only options matching the typed text.

Step Action: Verify the search results in the filter are relevant to the text entered.
Step Expected: Only matching product options are displayed in the filter list.

Step Action: Clear the search text and type into the Endpoint Type or Component Type filter search field.
Step Expected: The respective filter list narrows to matching options.

Step Action: Select a filtered result and apply it.
Step Expected: The table updates to reflect the selected filter value.

Step Action: Successfully verified that all filters on the Network Device Inventory Browse page are searchable and narrow results dynamically based on typed input.

Title: Network Device Inventory Browse - Global Single-Site Filter Restricts Site Filter Options

Step Action: Log in to OT Integrity Corp as a valid user.
Step Expected: User is authenticated and the application is displayed.

Step Action: Apply a global site filter for one specific site at the application level.
Step Expected: The global site filter is set to a single site.

Step Action: Navigate to the Network Device Inventory Browse page.
Step Expected: The Network Device Inventory Browse page is displayed.

Step Action: Locate the site filtering control on the page.
Step Expected: The site filter control is visible.

Step Action: Click the site filter to view available site options.
Step Expected: Only the single site selected in the global filter is displayed as an option in the site filter.

Step Action: Verify no other sites are selectable in the site filter.
Step Expected: Only the globally filtered site is available; all other sites are absent from the list.

Step Action: Verify the inventory table data reflects only the globally filtered site.
Step Expected: Table data contains network devices belonging only to the single globally filtered site.

Step Action: Successfully verified that when a global filter for one site is active the site filtering on the Network Device Inventory Browse page shows only that one site.

Title: Network Device Inventory Browse - Horizontal Scroll Keeps Endpoint and Component Number Columns Frozen

Step Action: Log in to OT Integrity Corp as a valid user.
Step Expected: User is authenticated and the application is displayed.

Step Action: Navigate to the Network Device Inventory Browse page.
Step Expected: The Network Device Inventory Browse page is displayed with the inventory table loaded.

Step Action: Ensure the browser window is sized so that horizontal scrolling is required to see all table columns.
Step Expected: A horizontal scrollbar is visible below the table.

Step Action: Note the position of the Endpoint column and the # (Number of Components) column before scrolling.
Step Expected: Both columns are visible on the left side of the table.

Step Action: Scroll the table horizontally to the right.
Step Expected: The table content moves to the right revealing additional columns.

Step Action: Verify the Endpoint column remains visible and frozen on the left side during horizontal scrolling.
Step Expected: The Endpoint column does not scroll out of view and remains frozen at the left.

Step Action: Verify the # (Number of Components) column remains visible and frozen adjacent to the Endpoint column during horizontal scrolling.
Step Expected: The # column does not scroll out of view and remains frozen.

Step Action: Scroll the table fully to the right to view the last columns and verify both frozen columns are still visible.
Step Expected: Endpoint and # columns remain frozen and visible regardless of horizontal scroll position.

Step Action: Successfully verified that the Endpoint column and # (Number of Components) column remain frozen during horizontal scrolling of the Network Device Inventory Browse table.

Title: Network Device Inventory Browse - Columns Are Sortable

Step Action: Log in to OT Integrity Corp as a valid user.
Step Expected: User is authenticated and the application is displayed.

Step Action: Navigate to the Network Device Inventory Browse page.
Step Expected: The Network Device Inventory Browse page is displayed with the inventory table loaded.

Step Action: Click the Endpoint column header to sort ascending.
Step Expected: The table rows re-order in ascending alphabetical or numerical order by Endpoint.

Step Action: Click the Endpoint column header again to sort descending.
Step Expected: The table rows re-order in descending order by Endpoint.

Step Action: Click the # (Number of Components) column header and verify sort.
Step Expected: The table rows re-order by number of components.

Step Action: Click the Manufacturer column header and verify sort.
Step Expected: The table rows re-order by Manufacturer.

Step Action: Click the Type column header and verify sort.
Step Expected: The table rows re-order by Type.

Step Action: Click the Product column header and verify sort.
Step Expected: The table rows re-order by Product.

Step Action: Click the Version column header and verify sort.
Step Expected: The table rows re-order by Version.

Step Action: Click the Integrity Asset column header and verify sort.
Step Expected: The table rows re-order by Integrity Asset.

Step Action: Successfully verified that all sortable columns (Endpoint; #; Manufacturer; Type; Product; Version; Integrity Asset) sort the inventory table correctly in both ascending and descending order.

Title: Network Device Inventory Browse - Conflicting Filters Display Empty Table with Message

Step Action: Log in to OT Integrity Corp as a valid user.
Step Expected: User is authenticated and the application is displayed.

Step Action: Navigate to the Network Device Inventory Browse page.
Step Expected: The Network Device Inventory Browse page is displayed with inventory data loaded.

Step Action: Select a specific Product filter value that exists in a known site.
Step Expected: The table updates to show endpoints matching the selected product.

Step Action: Without clearing the Product filter also select a Site filter for a site that does not have the selected product.
Step Expected: Both filter values are selected.

Step Action: Apply the combined filter selection.
Step Expected: The inventory table displays no results.

Step Action: Verify a message is displayed to the user indicating no results match the current filter combination.
Step Expected: A descriptive message (e.g. 'No results found' or similar) is displayed in the table area.

Step Action: Clear the conflicting filter and reapply only the Product filter.
Step Expected: The table repopulates with matching endpoint results.

Step Action: Successfully verified that selecting conflicting filters (a product and a site that does not carry that product) results in an empty table with a user-facing message.

Step Expected: User is authenticated and the application is displayed.

Step Action: Navigate to the Help section or click the Help icon within the application.
Step Expected: Help section is accessible and displayed.

Step Expected: Help topics related to Network Device Inventory Browse are listed.

Step Expected: Help topic content is displayed and is relevant to network device inventory browsing.

Step Expected: Help content explains Browse By functionality.

Step Expected: Help content describes filter usage including search capability and conflict handling.

Step Action: Verify the help topic describes the frozen columns behavior during horizontal scrolling.
Step Expected: Help content includes column freeze behavior.

Step Action: Successfully verified that help topic content for the Network Device Inventory Browse page has been created and is accessible and covers the key features of the page.

Title: Network Device Inventory Browse - Browse By Manufacturer with No Matching Filter Result

Step Action: Log in to OT Integrity Corp as a valid user.
Step Expected: User is authenticated and the application is displayed.

Step Action: Navigate to the Network Device Inventory Browse page.
Step Expected: The Network Device Inventory Browse page is displayed.

Step Action: Select 'Network Device Manufacturer' in Browse By.
Step Expected: The table displays endpoints filtered by manufacturer.

Step Action: Open the Product filter and search for a product name that does not exist in the inventory (e.g. a string of random characters).
Step Expected: The filter search returns no matching product options.

Step Action: Verify the filter search displays an empty result or a 'no results' message within the filter dropdown.
Step Expected: The filter shows no matching options with an appropriate indicator.

Step Action: Close the filter without making a selection.
Step Expected: No filter is applied.

Step Action: Select an Endpoint Type filter value that exists in the system.
Step Expected: The table updates to show only endpoints of that type.

Step Action: Additionally select a Product filter that is incompatible with the selected Endpoint Type.
Step Expected: The table displays no results.

Step Action: Verify a no-results message is displayed in the table.
Step Expected: A message is displayed indicating no data matches the current filter selections.

Step Action: Successfully verified that applying filters with no matching results in Browse By Manufacturer mode results in an empty table with a user-facing no-results message.

Title: Network Device Inventory Browse - Unauthenticated User Cannot Access Page

Step Action: Attempt to navigate directly to the Network Device Inventory Browse page URL without logging in.
Step Expected: User is redirected to the login page or an access-denied page is displayed.

Step Action: Verify the inventory table and its data are not accessible without authentication.
Step Expected: No inventory data is visible.

Step Action: Log in with valid credentials.
Step Expected: User is authenticated successfully.

Step Action: Navigate to the Network Device Inventory Browse page.
Step Expected: The page is displayed with the inventory table and data loaded.

Step Action: Successfully verified that the Network Device Inventory Browse page is not accessible to unauthenticated users and is accessible after valid login.
